explanation.rule: The suffix -ful is added to nouns to form adjectives meaning 'having' or 'full of' a particular quality.

groups.note: Added to nouns to form adjectives meaning 'having' or 'full of' a quality. Note: nouns ending in -y change to -i before -ful (beauty → beautiful).

examples.definition: helpful or having a practical purpose
giving attention to avoid mistakes or danger
willing to assist; providing useful support
very attractive or pleasing to the senses

wordForms.example: The country needs a powerful leader in difficult times.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the ability to control or influence people or things

wordForms.definition: helpful or having a practical purpose
wordForms.example: This guidebook is really useful for first-time visitors.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to employ something for a purpose

wordForms.definition: giving attention to avoid mistakes or danger
wordForms.example: Be careful when crossing the road at night.
wordForms.rootDefinition: attention given to avoid mistakes or danger

wordForms.definition: willing to assist; providing useful support
wordForms.example: The customer service team was very helpful.

wordForms.definition: very attractive or pleasing to the senses
wordForms.example: They got married on a beautiful summer evening.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the quality of being very attractive or pleasing

wordForms.example: Smoking is harmful to your health.
wordForms.rootDefinition: physical injury or damage to something

wordForms.definition: feeling or showing thanks for something received
wordForms.example: I am truly grateful for all your support.

wordForms.definition: feeling afraid or full of worry
wordForms.example: She was fearful of making another mistake.
wordForms.rootDefinition: an unpleasant feeling caused by danger or threat

wordForms.example: The village was a peaceful place to retire.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a state of calm and freedom from conflict

wordForms.example: We had a wonderful time on our holiday.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a feeling of surprise and admiration

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The new engine is very ___ and can run for hours without stopping.
Always be ___ when using sharp tools in the kitchen.
The staff at the hotel were extremely ___ during our stay.
Too much sun exposure can be ___ to your skin.
The view from the top of the hill was absolutely ___.
We spent a ___ evening by the lake watching the sunset.

transformExercises.sentence: The storm was so ___ that it knocked down several trees. (POWER)
She was deeply ___ for the help she received from her neighbours. (GRATE)
Please be ___ not to spill anything on the new carpet. (CARE)
The countryside looked ___ after the spring rain. (WONDER)
This app is very ___ for organising your daily schedule. (USE)
